body_md: Kickoff (2026-07-13): phase-1 harness validation done in one session. Next: (a) 004-dimension-sweep to test the eps/sqrt(d) scaling claim, (b) learned-model phase (logistic regression) where the targeted direction is estimated from weights, with multi-seed variance. Also: ingest the adversarial-examples prior art via reref discover before writing related work.

Ideation - problem & core idea (migrated from ideation.md; the plan now lives in this graph): Robustness numbers from RANDOM input noise are a weak proxy - in high dimensions an isotropic perturbation spends almost all of its budget in directions the decision function ignores, so a model can look robust to random noise yet be brittle to TARGETED perturbations of the same L2 budget. Core idea: fix budget eps, compare random (eps times a uniform direction) vs targeted (eps along the inter-class axis - the exact worst case for nearest-centroid) on the same classifier and data; geometry predicts margin erosion ~eps targeted vs ~eps/sqrt(d) random, so the gap should be large and grow with d. Start fully controlled (closed-form worst case) to validate the harness against theory before learned models.
